findings.title: Avoid copying protobuf keys before access-log formatting
findings.impact: When access logs are sampled in, including the default/all-1.0 configuration, protobuf-backed batch paths such as DeleteAllCopy and Exist now allocate and copy every key into a temporary std::vector<std::string> before formatting. Large batch requests pay O(n) extra heap churn on the worker request path even though access_recorder.cpp already adds an objectKeysToString overload for RepeatedPtrField.
findings.fix: Format the RepeatedPtrField directly in ObjectKeyState::FillObjectKey/FIllNestedKey by calling objectKeysToString(*protoRef), or preserve a summary-only protobuf path where the old code used ObjectKeysToAbbrStr.

findings.title: Avoid an intermediate string for every formatted access log
findings.impact: LogPerformance now calls FormatAccessReqMsg(), which builds or returns a full std::string and then copies it into the final log stream. The previous helper appended directly to the existing stream, so sampled-in/default access logs now pay an extra allocation and copy proportional to the request-message size.
findings.fix: Keep FormatAccessReqMsg as an append-style helper that writes directly into the caller's std::ostringstream, or add an overload that appends the sample marker to the existing log stream without materializing an intermediate string.
